source_lookup.docs_root_note: All doc_path values are relative to the skill directory that contains this catalog.

campfire.notes: Use only for targeted implementation examples after the user selects a practice.

fizzy.notes: Use only for targeted implementation examples after the user selects a practice.

import_modes.docs_only: Copy or adapt the selected reference docs into the target Rails repo without broad architecture rewrites.
import_modes.starter_scaffold: Set up a new or mostly-empty Rails app around the chosen practices with small starter files and structure.
import_modes.guided_patch: Patch an existing Rails app carefully after inspecting the current code, dependencies, routes, and test shape.

selection_axes.question: Is the application single-account or multi-tenant?
selection_axes.why_it_matters: This decides the core auth and tenant model, and it is the one branch that should not be merged casually.

selection_axes.question: Will the app serve mostly HTML, or HTML plus Turbo Streams and JSON endpoints from the same resources?
selection_axes.why_it_matters: This decides whether the base controller guidance is enough or whether hybrid resource controllers should be part of the initial shape.

selection_axes.question: Does the app need presence, unread state, or live multi-user collaboration?
selection_axes.why_it_matters: Campfire's collaboration documents are strongest when the app behaves like a shared workspace instead of a classic CRUD app.

selection_axes.question: Will the project use deliberate server-rendered UI with custom CSS, or does it already have a separate design system or Tailwind stack?
selection_axes.why_it_matters: Both source projects lean on ERB, helpers, Stimulus, and custom CSS; that philosophy transfers well, but CSS architecture should not be copied blindly into a repo that already standardized on another stack.

bundles.label: Mixed Rails Foundation
bundles.summary: A portable base for a fresh Rails app: domain-first models, small controllers, disciplined views, and integration-heavy tests.
recommended_for: new Rails apps that need a strong default architecture
teams that want a shared baseline before adding tenancy or realtime

bundles.label: Campfire Collaboration Stack
bundles.summary: Single-account, collaboration-heavy Rails with memberships, realtime updates, rich content, and classic Rails primitives.
recommended_for: shared inbox, chat, CRM, support, or workspace products

bundles.label: Fizzy SaaS Stack
bundles.summary: Multi-tenant Rails with passwordless auth, tenant-aware authorization, hybrid controllers, operational patterns, and Rails-first UI.
recommended_for: multi-account apps that need structured auth and portability

bundles.label: Hotwire UI Layer
bundles.summary: Server-rendered UI patterns for teams that want strong partials, custom CSS, form components, and pragmatic Turbo usage.
recommended_for: teams standardizing on ERB, Stimulus, and custom CSS

bundles.label: Operations and Portability
bundles.summary: Operational patterns for background work, events, exports, platform awareness, and production-oriented Rails concerns.
recommended_for: teams that need exports, notifications, or platform-sensitive behavior

families.label: Auth and Tenancy
families.guidance: Choose exactly one core tenancy track. Campfire is intentionally single-account and session-centric. Fizzy is intentionally multi-tenant and tenant-aware. Do not import both bootstraps into the same app.

overlaps.guidance: Both define the core auth stack. Campfire assumes cookie-backed sessions for a single-account app. Fizzy assumes passwordless auth in a multi-tenant system.
These are mutually exclusive starting points. Choose Campfire for single-account installs and Fizzy for account membership systems.
These two Fizzy docs are meant to travel together.

families.label: Controllers and Routes
families.guidance: Pick one base resource-controller philosophy, then layer in hybrid responses only if the app truly serves HTML, Turbo Streams, and JSON from the same resources.

overlaps.guidance: These are strongly aligned. Choose one canonical controller doc to avoid duplicate doctrine.
Hybrid controllers are an extension of the base resourceful pattern, not a replacement for it.

families.label: Models and Domain
families.guidance: These documents mostly compose well. Use domain-driven design and concerns as the foundation, then add state, callbacks, or delegated types where the domain calls for them.

overlaps.guidance: These are the same organizational instinct from two codebases. Pick one as the main concern doctrine.

families.label: Views, CSS, and Forms
families.guidance: Choose one base view philosophy doc, then add CSS architecture and form components if the target app wants a custom server-rendered UI system.

overlaps.guidance: Both push toward partials, helpers, Stimulus, and restraint. Use one as the base doctrine and draw examples from the other if needed.
Form components sit naturally on top of the CSS architecture.

families.label: Realtime and Collaboration
families.guidance: Choose Campfire when the app needs collaboration objects, membership boundaries, presence, or unread state. Choose Fizzy when the app mostly needs reusable Turbo patterns without the full collaboration domain.

overlaps.guidance: Campfire is the stronger collaboration-system reference. Fizzy is the stronger generic Turbo pattern catalog. They can complement each other, but Campfire should anchor live collaboration apps.
These two Campfire docs usually travel together.

families.label: Content, Storage, and Profiles
families.guidance: These practices generally compose well. Rich content, attachment authorization, and profile handling solve adjacent concerns and can be imported together.

families.label: Jobs, Events, and Integrations
families.guidance: Campfire's job discipline and Fizzy's event and advanced operational patterns complement each other well.

families.label: Testing
families.guidance: Both testing docs are closely aligned. Pick one as the canonical testing reference and only pull the second if you need concrete examples from both codebases.

overlaps.guidance: The testing philosophy is nearly identical across both projects.

families.label: Portability and Platform
families.guidance: These Fizzy practices are specialized operational add-ons. Pull them only if the target app needs exports, imports, platform detection, or request-context features.

campfire:authentication-and-current.title: Authentication and Current Context
campfire:authentication-and-current.summary: Cookie-backed sessions, narrow Current usage, and signed-link patterns for single-account apps.

campfire:background-jobs-and-integration.title: Background Jobs and Integrations
campfire:background-jobs-and-integration.summary: Thin jobs, explicit integration boundaries, and clear failure behavior.

campfire:memberships-and-room-architectu.title: Memberships and Room Architecture
campfire:memberships-and-room-architectu.summary: Collaboration architecture built around first-class membership records and per-user state.

campfire:model-organization-with-concern.title: Model Organization with Concerns
campfire:model-organization-with-concern.summary: Trait-based concerns and small collaborators for large Active Record models.

campfire:realtime-with-hotwire-and-actio.title: Realtime with Hotwire and Action Cable
campfire:realtime-with-hotwire-and-actio.summary: Live collaboration patterns that separate persisted HTML updates from ephemeral signals.

campfire:resourceful-controller-design.title: Resourceful Controller Design
campfire:resourceful-controller-design.summary: Small controllers and noun-based routes built around resources instead of ad hoc verbs.

campfire:rich-content-and-attachments.title: Rich Content and Attachments
campfire:rich-content-and-attachments.summary: Canonical rich-body modeling, attachment safety, and projection-friendly content handling.

campfire:single-account-bootstrap.title: Single-Account Bootstrap
campfire:single-account-bootstrap.summary: Explicit bootstrap flow for apps that are installed once per customer or run as one shared account.

campfire:testing-philosophy.title: Testing Philosophy
campfire:testing-philosophy.summary: Integration-heavy Rails testing with focused browser coverage and real-stack confidence.

campfire:view-layer-and-css.title: View Layer and CSS
campfire:view-layer-and-css.summary: Partials, helpers, Stimulus wiring, and custom CSS as the main server-rendered UI stack.

fizzy:account-data-transfer.title: Structured Account Data Transfer
fizzy:account-data-transfer.summary: Ordered export and import of account data using record groups and manifests.

fizzy:active-storage-authorization.title: Active Storage Authorization
fizzy:active-storage-authorization.summary: Authorize files through the records that own them instead of ad hoc blob whitelists.

fizzy:advanced-rails-patterns.title: Advanced Rails Patterns
fizzy:advanced-rails-patterns.summary: Production-grade patterns for jobs, storage, search, notifications, webhooks, and related operational concerns.

fizzy:authentication-spec.title: Passwordless Authentication Architecture
fizzy:authentication-spec.summary: Passwordless auth architecture with sessions, tokens, and tenant-aware authorization boundaries.

fizzy:callbacks-done-right.title: Callbacks Done Right
fizzy:callbacks-done-right.summary: A pragmatic callback discipline: use them when they fit the lifecycle, not as hidden service layers.

fizzy:concerns-for-organization.title: Using Rails Concerns for Organization
fizzy:concerns-for-organization.summary: Use concerns as real traits and organizational units instead of dumping behavior into services.

fizzy:delegated-types-pattern.title: The Delegated Types Pattern
fizzy:delegated-types-pattern.summary: Use delegated types when several content variants share operational behavior but need distinct internals.

fizzy:domain-driven-design.title: Domain-Driven Design
fizzy:domain-driven-design.summary: Put the domain model at the center of the app and let controllers and views stay thin around it.

fizzy:event-sourcing-and-activity.title: Event Sourcing and Activity Tracking
fizzy:event-sourcing-and-activity.summary: A single event model for feeds, notifications, webhooks, and audit-style activity.

fizzy:form-components.title: Professional Form Components in Rails
fizzy:form-components.summary: Consistent, accessible form components built with CSS custom properties and Stimulus behaviors.

fizzy:hybrid-resource-controllers.title: Hybrid Resource Controllers
fizzy:hybrid-resource-controllers.summary: Serve HTML, Turbo Stream, and JSON from the same resource controller without fragmenting the domain.

fizzy:modern-css-architecture.title: Modern CSS Architecture Without a Build Step
fizzy:modern-css-architecture.summary: Layered custom CSS architecture for Rails without Tailwind, Sass, or a CSS build step.

fizzy:multi-tenant-authentication.title: Multi-Tenant Authentication and Authorization
fizzy:multi-tenant-authentication.summary: Identity, membership, tenant resolution, and layered authorization for multi-tenant Rails apps.

fizzy:platform-aware-rails.title: Platform-Aware Rails
fizzy:platform-aware-rails.summary: Treat request metadata, timezone, and client platform as first-class Rails inputs.

fizzy:restful-resource-design.title: RESTful Resource Design
fizzy:restful-resource-design.summary: CRUD-oriented resource design that keeps controllers small by modeling endpoints as nouns.

fizzy:state-modeling-with-records.title: State Modeling with Records
fizzy:state-modeling-with-records.summary: Model optional or significant state with associated records when booleans stop carrying enough meaning.

fizzy:testing-philosophy.title: Testing Philosophy
fizzy:testing-philosophy.summary: Integration-heavy Rails testing with selective smaller tests for dense domain logic.

fizzy:turbo-patterns.title: Turbo and Hotwire Patterns
fizzy:turbo-patterns.summary: Reusable Turbo patterns for real-time Rails UIs, multi-session updates, and light abstraction.

fizzy:user-profiles-and-avatars.title: User Profiles and Avatars
fizzy:user-profiles-and-avatars.summary: Profiles, avatars, preferences, theming, and user-boundary decisions around personal data.

fizzy:view-layer-philosophy.title: View Layer Philosophy
fizzy:view-layer-philosophy.summary: Partials and helpers as the default Rails view abstraction, with presenter objects as the rare exception.
